meta.summary: cooltools 0.7.1 command-line and Python runtime for analyzing Hi-C and Micro-C contact maps stored in .cool/.mcool format.
meta.description: cooltools 0.7.1 command-line and Python runtime for analyzing Hi-C and Micro-C contact maps stored in .cool/.mcool format.

test: set -eu; cooltools --version 2>&1 | grep -F '0.7.1' >/dev/null; python -c 'import importlib.metadata as md; import cooltools; print(md.version("cooltools"))' | grep -Fx '0.7.1' >/dev/null; cooltools --help 2>&1 | grep -F 'expected-cis' >/dev/null; cooltools --help 2>&1 | grep -F 'insulation' >/dev/null; cooltools --help 2>&1 | grep -F 'genome' >/dev/null

set -eu; python -c 'import bioframe, cooler, cooltools, click, h5py, joblib, matplotlib, multiprocess, numba, numpy, pandas, scipy, skimage, sklearn; print(cooltools.__version__)' | grep -Fx '0.7.1' >/dev/null; cooler --version 2>&1 | grep -F 'cooler' >/dev/null

set -eu; cooltools coverage --help 2>&1 | grep -F 'Calculate the sums of cis' >/dev/null; cooltools expected-cis --help 2>&1 | grep -F 'Calculate expected Hi-C signal' >/dev/null; cooltools genome binnify --help 2>&1 | grep -F 'CHROMSIZES_PATH' >/dev/null

set -eu; tmp=/tmp/taf-cooltools-coverage-$$; rm -rf "$tmp"; mkdir -p "$tmp"; python /opt/cooltools/share/testdata/cooltools_smoke.py "$tmp"; cooler info "$tmp/tiny.cool" | grep -F 'nbins' >/dev/null; cooltools coverage --ignore-diags 0 "$tmp/tiny.cool" -o "$tmp/coverage.tsv"; test -s "$tmp/coverage.tsv"; grep -F 'cov_cis_raw' "$tmp/coverage.tsv" >/dev/null; grep -F 'chr1' "$tmp/coverage.tsv" >/dev/null; rm -rf "$tmp"

set -eu; tmp=/tmp/taf-cooltools-genome-$$; rm -rf "$tmp"; mkdir -p "$tmp"; printf 'chr1\t3000\nchr2\t2000\n' > "$tmp/chrom.sizes"; cooltools genome binnify --all-names "$tmp/chrom.sizes" 1000 > "$tmp/bins.bed"; test -s "$tmp/bins.bed"; grep -F 'chr1' "$tmp/bins.bed" >/dev/null; grep -F 'chr2' "$tmp/bins.bed" >/dev/null; rm -rf "$tmp"

upstream.name: cooltools
upstream.citation: Open2C et al. Cooltools: enabling high-resolution Hi-C analysis in Python. bioRxiv (2022).
